steps.title: Identify the clause structures
steps.content: The blank comes between “This is a hypothetical ______” (completing an independent clause) and “Proto-Indo-European is only postulated, its characteristics being inferred from the linguistic properties of its 448 daughters” (an independent clause with an attached participial phrase). The word “however” must bridge these two independent clauses.

steps.title: Apply the rule for conjunctive adverbs
steps.content: The pattern for joining independent clauses with a conjunctive adverb is “IC; however, IC”—semicolon before, comma after. Choice C (designation; however,) follows this exactly. The other choices violate the rule by either lacking required punctuation (A), creating two sentences (B), or reversing the punctuation order (D). That matches choice C — C is correct.
